name: Dependability Co School
recent_news: NLRB cases 04-RC-117368 and 04-CA-119117 (2013-2014) involve Dependability Company LLC at 3955 Frankford Ave employing drivers, attendants, mechanics and dispatchers; intrastate passenger carrier per FMCSA (USDOT 1734644).
fit_notes: Independent Philly-area school bus contractor with ~24 buses likely serving School District of Philadelphia. Small but in Summit's home market; possible tuck-in if not already part of a roll-up. Low online footprint warrants direct outreach.

name: Toney's School Bus Co
districts_or_customers: Schuyler County R-I School District (Lancaster, MO)
recent_news: Founder Kenneth O. Toney passed away February 2015; Toney Bus Company drivers served as honorary pallbearers. Company appears to continue as small family operation servicing rural Schuyler R-I.
fit_notes: Very small rural northern Missouri operator (~5 buses) with founder deceased and likely succession dynamics. Too small to be a meaningful tuck-in on its own; only attractive as part of a broader rural MO consolidation.

name: Community Connections Trnsprtn
recent_news: Founded 2015 by Kimber Saint-Preux; FDOT-certified paratransit provider serving metro Orlando; donates up to 10% of profits to senior/disabled causes.
fit_notes: Mission-driven senior/paratransit operator, NOT a school-bus contractor. Wrong vertical for Summit (no school district contracts identified). Skip unless Summit expands into NEMT/senior paratransit.

name: Advanced Care Transportation
recent_news: Per BBB profile, the business is reported as 'out of business' although website remained active; operated as ACT Express, Inc. providing wheelchair/stretcher L-Van paratransit across Georgia.
fit_notes: Likely defunct or wind-down NEMT/paratransit operator, not a school-bus contractor. Wrong vertical and questionable going-concern status. Skip.

name: C Transit Inc
recent_news: No public news; listed website c-transit.com is unreachable. Small Brooklyn-based operator at 1626 Nostrand Ave; not confirmed in NYC DOE OPT vendor lists in available sources.
fit_notes: Very small Brooklyn special-needs/ambulette operator with minimal digital footprint and unverifiable status. Likely sub-scale for Summit tuck-in; would need direct outreach to confirm NYC DOE contracts and going-concern.
